summary: Create gui/src/components/ directory for shared Splitter component; Write failing tests for Splitter component: renders a draggable splitter bar with data-testid, applies correct orientation (horizontal/vertical), mouse drag updates callback with delta position, respects min/max size constraints, has appropriate cursor styling.; Implement Splitter component (gui/src/components/Splitter.tsx + Splitter.module.css): a thin draggable bar that fires onResize(delta) on mousemove during drag. Props: orientation ('horizontal'|'vertical'), onResize callback, data-testid. Handles mousedown/mousemove/mouseup lifecycle, sets cursor on document during drag.; Write failing tests for wired App layout — renders all real components: test that App mounts and contains viewport-canvas (from Viewport), editor-container (from Editor), property-editor (from PropertyEditor), constraint-panel (from ConstraintPanel), toolbar (from Toolbar), status-bar (from StatusBar). Must mock bridge (getInitialState, event listeners) and Three.js/Viewport internals. Verify toolbar is at top and status bar at bottom via DOM order.; Rewrite App.tsx to compose all real components into the layout: create engineStore, editorStore, selectionStore. Render Toolbar at top, 3-column main area (Editor left, Viewport center, PropertyEditor+ConstraintPanel right), StatusBar at bottom. Pass store data as props to each component. Wire setParameter callback through bridge.setParameter. Wire toolbar onExport/onFitToView stubs.; Write failing tests for resizable splitters in App: verify two Splitter components exist between left-center and center-right columns. Test that dragging a splitter updates panel widths (via CSS custom properties or inline styles).; Add resizable panel layout to App: integrate Splitter components between editor/viewport and viewport/side-panel. Track panel widths with createSignal, update on Splitter onResize. Apply widths via grid-template-columns with CSS custom properties. Update App.module.css for the full grid layout with toolbar row, main resizable area, and status bar row.; Write failing test for initial state loading on mount: mock bridge.getInitialState to return test GuiState (meshes, values, constraints, files). Verify that after mount, engineStore is populated (values appear in PropertyEditor), editorStore has files open, and subscribeToEvents was called.

Fix test parse errors (JSX in vi.mock factories), add beforeEach mock reset, implement reactive document.title (createEffect watching activeFile + evalStatus), add horizontal Splitter in side panel between PropertyEditor and ConstraintPanel with draggable resize. All 25 tests pass (7 Splitter + 18 App). All 12 plan steps now complete.

Fix bridge.ts exportGeometry: change signature from (entityPath, outputPath) to (format, outputPath), update invoke call from 'export_geometry' to 'export' with {format, path: outputPath} params to match the backend command handler. This is a breaking change but handleExport() in App.tsx is currently a stub so nothing depends on the old signature.; Add ExportFormat type to types.ts: type ExportFormat = 'step' | 'stl' | '3mf'. Add FileEntry interface for file browser tree: { path: string; name: string; isDirectory: boolean; children?: FileEntry[] }. Add ToastMessage interface: { id: string; type: 'success' | 'error' | 'info'; message: string }.; Create gui/src/hooks/ directory for reusable hook functions (useKeyboardShortcuts).

All 12 plan steps complete. Steps 1-11 were already implemented in prior commits (c4d8a95..2148af2) but plan.json was not updated. Step 12 (wire all new components into App.tsx) was uncommitted — committed as e548b70. Verified all 221 tests pass across 25 test files. Components: Toast, FileBrowser, ExportDialog, useKeyboardShortcuts hook, ReloadPrompt, panels smoke test, and full App integration with ExportDialog state, keyboard shortcuts, file-changed event subscription, and reload prompt.

Write failing tests for Toast component in __tests__/Toast.test.tsx: (1) renders with data-testid='toast', (2) displays message text from props, (3) applies correct data-type attribute for 'success' and 'error' types, (4) renders a close button, (5) clicking close button calls onDismiss callback, (6) hidden when message is null/empty.; Implement Toast component in panels/Toast.tsx + Toast.module.css. Props: { message: string; type: 'success' | 'error' | 'info'; onDismiss: () => void }. Uses data-testid='toast', data-type attribute for styling, close button. Add export to panels/index.ts.; Write failing tests for FileBrowser in __tests__/FileBrowser.test.tsx: (1) renders with data-testid='file-browser', (2) renders a list item for each file in files prop, (3) displays basename of file path (not full path), (4) the item matching activeFile prop has data-active='true' attribute, (5) clicking a file item calls onFileClick(path), (6) shows 'No files' empty state when files array is empty.; Implement FileBrowser component in panels/FileBrowser.tsx + FileBrowser.module.css. Props: { files: FileData[]; activeFile: string | null; onFileClick: (path: string) => void }. Uses For to iterate files, Show for empty state. Each entry shows basename, has data-active attribute when matching activeFile, click calls onFileClick. Add export to panels/index.ts.; Write failing tests for ExportDialog in __tests__/ExportDialog.test.tsx: (1) renders modal with data-testid='export-dialog' when open=true, (2) not in DOM when open=false, (3) format selector has STEP/STL/3MF options, (4) default selected format is 'step', (5) clicking Export button calls onExport(selectedFormat), (6) clicking Cancel calls onClose, (7) changing format selector updates selection, (8) shows progress indicator when exporting=true prop set.; Implement ExportDialog component in panels/ExportDialog.tsx + ExportDialog.module.css. Props: { open: boolean; exporting: boolean; onExport: (format: ExportFormat) => void; onClose: () => void }. Modal overlay with format <select>, Export/Cancel buttons, progress indicator. Uses Show for conditional rendering. Add export to panels/index.ts.; Write failing tests for useKeyboardShortcuts in __tests__/useKeyboardShortcuts.test.ts: (1) dispatching Ctrl+O keydown on document calls onOpen callback, (2) dispatching F5 keydown calls onReEvaluate, (3) dispatching Ctrl+E keydown calls onExportDialog, (4) dispatching Ctrl+O when target is an <input> does NOT call onOpen (avoids conflict), (5) after cleanup, dispatching shortcuts does nothing (listeners removed).; Implement useKeyboardShortcuts hook in hooks/useKeyboardShortcuts.ts. Accepts callbacks: { onOpen?: () => void; onReEvaluate?: () => void; onExportDialog?: () => void }. Registers document keydown listener on mount (via SolidJS onMount), removes on cleanup (onCleanup). Maps Ctrl+O, F5, Ctrl+E. Skips if event target is input/textarea/[contenteditable].; Write failing tests for ReloadPrompt in __tests__/ReloadPrompt.test.tsx: (1) renders banner with data-testid='reload-prompt' when filePath prop is non-null, (2) not in DOM when filePath is null, (3) displays the basename of filePath in the message, (4) clicking Reload button calls onReload, (5) clicking Dismiss button calls onDismiss.; Implement ReloadPrompt component in panels/ReloadPrompt.tsx + ReloadPrompt.module.css. Props: { filePath: string | null; onReload: () => void; onDismiss: () => void }. Thin banner above editor: '{basename} changed on disk. Reload?' with Reload and Dismiss buttons. Uses Show for conditional rendering. Add export to panels/index.ts.; Update panels-smoke.test.tsx to include all new components: add FileBrowser (with files=[] and onFileClick=vi.fn()), ExportDialog (with open=true, exporting=false, onExport=vi.fn(), onClose=vi.fn()), Toast (with message/type/onDismiss), ReloadPrompt (with filePath/onReload/onDismiss). Verify all data-testid attributes present.; Wire all new components into App.tsx and update layout: (1) Add FileBrowser in editor panel showing editorStore.state.openFiles, wire onFileClick to setActiveFile, (2) Add ExportDialog state (showExportDialog signal), wire handleExport to open it, wire onExport to call bridge.exportGeometry with format + native save dialog path (mock-safe), show Toast on success/error, (3) Wire useKeyboardShortcuts: Ctrl+O opens file via bridge.openFile, F5 re-evaluates by calling updateSource, Ctrl+E toggles export dialog, (4) Subscribe to bridge.onFileChanged in onMount, track changedFile signal, show ReloadPrompt for open files, wire Reload to update content, Dismiss to clear prompt, (5) Update App.module.css with fileBrowser class for layout, (6) Update App.test.tsx bridge mock to include onFileChanged. Ensure all new components render in App smoke tests.

Added 4 failing tests for ExportDialog concurrent-invocation guard (Export button disabled, Cancel button disabled, Export button enabled when not exporting, format selector disabled). Then fixed ExportDialog.tsx by adding disabled={props.exporting} to Export button, Cancel button, and format <select>. All 225 tests pass across 25 test files. All plan steps now complete.

Complete task 98: Tauri bundler + CLI integration + distribution. Generated placeholder icon files (PNG/ICO/ICNS) for Tauri v2 bundler. Added 'gui <file>' subcommand to reify-cli that validates file existence and .ri extension, then spawns the reify-gui binary. Configured tauri.conf.json with bundle.active=true, cross-platform targets (deb/appimage/dmg/msi/nsis), icon paths, .ri file associations, and OCCT shared library resources. Added OCCT bundling documentation. All 15 CLI tests pass (3 smoke + 6 gui + 2 lsp + 2 lsp_protocol + 2 build).
